ip_outcome_note: No IP flag. Item card depicting a scroll artifact.

trivia_bullets.text: Battle Styles (March 19, 2021) introduced the Single Strike and Rapid Strike battle styles as the set's defining mechanic. Single Strike cards are color-coded red; Rapid Strike in blue. Single Strike Scroll of Scorn is one of the Tool cards designed to anchor Single Strike-style decks. [Pokemon.com: Battle Styles Single Strike and Rapid Strike announcement, via WebSearch; Bulbapedia: Battle Styles TCG, via WebSearch]
sources: Pokemon.com: Battle Styles Single Strike and Rapid Strike announcement

trivia_bullets.text: As a Pokemon Tool, Scroll of Scorn grants the attached Single Strike Pokemon access to the Furious Anger attack (Fighting Energy, 10+ damage — plus 10 per damage counter on that Pokemon). The attack scales with damage taken, rewarding aggressive high-damage trades where the attacker is also taking hits. [Bulbapedia: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn (Battle Styles 133), via WebSearch; Pojo.com review, via WebSearch]
sources: Bulbapedia: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn (Battle Styles 133)
Pojo.com: Single Strike Scroll of Scorn review

trivia_bullets.text: Illustrated by 5ban Graphics, the studio responsible for the majority of Pokemon TCG V and VMAX card art in the Sword & Shield era. The scroll aesthetic draws on Japanese martial arts imagery — a sealed scroll of techniques is a common device in fighting-arts lore signifying inherited knowledge or forbidden techniques. [Frontmatter artist field; Serebii TCG cardex]

related_cards.name: Urn of Vitality
related_cards.note: Companion Single Strike item card in the same set — recovers Single Strike Energy from discard

related_cards.name: Single Strike Urshifu V
related_cards.note: Flagship Single Strike Legendary for the expansion

related_cards.name: Single Strike Energy
related_cards.note: The energy type Scroll of Scorn decks run; interacts with Urn of Vitality recovery
